HOOK · Heating / Cooling Curves
加热 / 冷却曲线
HOOK
•
A hot drink left on a table cools down.
桌上放着的一杯热饮会变凉。
•
If you measured its temperature every minute and plotted the numbers, you would draw a cooling curve.
如果你每分钟量一次温度,把数字画出来,就画出了一条冷却曲线。
•
Scientists use these temperature-time curves to find the exact temperature at which a substance melts or boils, and to see when a change of state is happening.
科学家用这种温度-时间曲线,找出一种物质熔化或沸腾的确切温度,也能看出什么时候正在发生状态变化。
•
This lesson asks: why does the line go flat at certain points, even while you keep heating or cooling?
这一课问:为什么你一直在加热或冷却,线却会在某些点变平?
LEARN · Heating / Cooling Curves
加热 / 冷却曲线
LEARN
Reading the curve
•
A heating curve plots temperature (up the side) against time (along the bottom) as a substance is heated steadily.
加热曲线把温度(竖轴)对时间(横轴)画出来,显示一种物质被稳定加热时的情况。
•
A cooling curve does the same as a substance is cooled.
冷却曲线一样,显示物质被冷却时的情况。
•
A sloping section means the temperature is changing.
斜的一段表示温度在变化。
•
A flat section (a plateau) means the temperature stays the same even though heating or cooling continues.
平的一段(平台)表示就算还在加热或冷却,温度也保持不变。
Why the plateau happens
•
During a plateau, the substance is changing state (e.g.
在平台段,物质正在改变状态(例如熔化或沸腾)。
•
melting or boiling).
提供的能量用来克服粒子之间的力,而不是用来升高温度,所以温度保持不变,直到变化完成。
•
The energy supplied is used to overcome the forces between the particles, not to raise the temperature, so the temperature stays constant until the change is complete.
熔点是固体变液体的那个平台。
•
The melting point is the plateau where solid changes to liquid.
沸点是液体变气体的那个平台。
•
The boiling point is the plateau where liquid changes to gas.
🔴 RED CARD · 误区红牌
✘
If the line is flat, no energy is being added and nothing is happening.
如果线是平的,就表示没有加能量、什么都没发生。
✔
Energy is still being added during a plateau. It is being used to change the state of the substance, not to raise the temperature.
平台段时仍然在加能量。这些能量用来改变物质的状态,而不是用来 升高温度。
Safety + boundary
•
Heating and boiling-water experiments must be supervised by a teacher.
安全:加热和沸水实验必须有老师看着。
•
Wear eye protection and keep hands away from hot apparatus.
戴护目镜,手远离烫的仪器。
•
NOTE (for later study): the exact amount of energy needed during a plateau can be calculated with a formula.
提醒(以后才学):平台段需要的确切能量可以用一个公式算出来。
•
That calculation is studied in a later year, not here.
那个计算以后年级才学,这里不学。
•
In this lesson we only read and describe the curve.
这一课只读图和描述曲线。
TRY · Heating / Cooling Curves
加热 / 冷却曲线
TRY
Q01Name / Describe / Complete5 marks
Oliver investigates change of state. He puts crushed ice into a beaker and heats it gently. Every 2 minutes Oliver records the temperature using apparatus X. (a) What is the name of apparatus X? .................. [1] (b) The table shows Oliver's results. (i) Describe the pattern in these results after the first 2 minutes. .................. [1] (ii) Which result does not fit this pattern? temperature .................. deg C [1] (c) Complete these sentences. In the first 2 minutes ice changes state from a .................. to a .................. . In the next two minutes the particles gain more .................. and move .................. . [2]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) Thermometer. (b)(i) After the first 2 minutes, temperature increases as time increases. (b)(ii) 50°C. (c) Ice changes from solid to liquid; particles gain energy and move faster / further apart.
(a) 温度计。(b)(i) 第一个 2 分钟之后，时间越长，温度越高。(b)(ii) 50°C。(c) 冰从固体变成液体；粒子得到能量，动得更快 / 距离变远。
Accept · 可收：
  • thermometer
  • temperature probe
  • temperature increases as time increases
  • temperature keeps rising after the first 2 minutes
  • 50°C
  • 50 deg C
  • solid to liquid
  • ice melts to water
Q02State / Explain3 marks
Sanjeet investigates changes of state. He puts ice cubes into a beaker and leaves them to melt. Every 5 minutes Sanjeet measures the temperature. He keeps measuring until the water reaches room temperature. The graph shows his results. (a) What is the temperature of the room? .................. deg C [1] (b) Use the particle theory to explain what is happening to the water particles between 10 minutes and 20 minutes. [2]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) 20 deg C; (b) particles gain energy / kinetic energy; move faster / more /; further apart.
(a) 20 deg C; (b) particles gain energy / kinetic energy; move faster / more /; further apart.
Accept · 可收：
  • (a) 20 deg C
  • (b) particles gain energy / kinetic energy
  • move faster / more /
  • further apart
  • (a) 20 deg C; (b) particles gain energy / kinetic energy; move faster / more /; further apart
  • (a) 20 deg C.
  • (b) 粒子获得能量 / kinetic energy。
Q03State / Describe3 marks
Mia makes a thermometer. Look at the apparatus she uses. (a) Mia measures the height, h, of the liquid in the glass tube. Mia then places the boiling tube into a beaker of water and ice. (i) What happens to the height of the liquid in the glass tube? .................. [1] (ii) Describe how the movement and arrangement of water particles change as the water gets colder. .................. [2]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a)(i) the height of the liquid falls / goes down; (a)(ii) any two: lose energy / move slower; particles get closer; together; arrangement becomes more ordered / less random.
(a)(i) the height of the liquid falls / goes down; (a)(ii) any two: lose energy / move slower; particles get closer; together; arrangement becomes more ordered / less random.
Accept · 可收：
  • (a)(i) the height of the liquid falls / goes down
  • (a)(ii) any two: lose energy / move slower
  • particles get closer
  • together
  • arrangement becomes more ordered / less random
  • (a)(i) the height of the liquid falls / goes down; (a)(ii) any two: lose energy / move slower; particles get closer; together; arrangement becomes more ordered / less random
  • (a)(i) the height of the liquid falls / goes down.
Q04Record / Read1 mark
Angelique measures the temperature of three samples of water. The diagrams show her results. Record the temperature of each thermometer in the table.
Expected answer · 标准答案
A = 0.0 deg C.
A = 0.0 deg C.
Accept · 可收：
  • A = 0.0 deg C
  • B = -11.0 deg C
  • C = 32.5 deg C
  • (accept: 0
  • -11)
  • 3 correct = 2
  • 2 correct = 1
  • 0 or 1 = 0
TEST · Heating / Cooling Curves
加热 / 冷却曲线
EXAM MODE · NO HINTS · 考试模式
Q05Circle / Describe / Sketch2 marks
The diagram shows a cooling curve for a pure substance. (a) Which word describes the process X on the cooling curve? Circle the correct answer. boiling condensing freezing melting (b) Describe two things that happen to the particles of the substance during freezing. 1 .................. 2 .................. [2] (c) A solid is heated until it becomes a gas. Sketch a graph to show how the temperature changes during this process. Use the axes provided. [RESERVED IMAGE SLOT — model-answer sketch graph, no source diagram supplied; to be drawn at build]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) condensing; (b) any two: lose energy; slow down; more ordered / less random /; lattice; closer / tightly packed; attractive forces increase; (c) increasing graph with two horizontal sections (plateaus).
(a) condensing; (b) any two: lose energy; slow down; more ordered / less random /; lattice; closer / tightly packed; attractive forces increase; (c) increasing graph with two horizontal sections (plateaus).
Accept · 可收：
  • (a) condensing
  • (b) any two: lose energy
  • slow down
  • more ordered / less random /
  • lattice
  • closer / tightly packed
  • attractive forces increase
  • (c) increasing graph with two horizontal sections (plateaus)
Q06Write / Draw2 marks
(a) The table contains descriptions for each letter in the diagram. Write the correct letter next to each description. One has been done for you. [2] (b) The diagram shows the particles at point H on the cooling curve. Draw the particles at point F in this box.
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) solid = K; condensing = G; freezing = J; liquid = H; gas = F (given); (b) draw 2-12 gas particles, not in a pattern, not touching; (accept: 2-6 molecules; ignore arrows / different sizes).
(a) solid = K; condensing = G; freezing = J; liquid = H; gas = F (given); (b) draw 2-12 gas particles, not in a pattern, not touching; (accept: 2-6 molecules; ignore arrows / different sizes).
Accept · 可收：
  • (a) solid = K
  • condensing = G
  • freezing = J
  • liquid = H
  • gas = F (given)
  • (b) draw 2-12 gas particles, not in a pattern, not touching
  • (accept: 2-6 molecules
  • ignore arrows / different sizes)
Q07Tick / State / Complete / Draw10 marks
This question is about liquids. (a) When a liquid is put into a beaker it takes on the shape of the beaker. Why do liquids take on the shape of the beaker? Tick the boxes next to two correct answers. Liquids have no fixed shape. [ ] Liquids have no fixed volume. [ ] Liquid particles only vibrate. [ ] The particles in a liquid expand. [ ] The particles in a liquid move around. [ ] [2] (b) Pierre investigates the boiling point of water. He heats pure water until it boils and measures the boiling point, repeating with new pure-water samples, then three more times with salt water. The table shows some results. (i) There is one result missing. The thermometer shows Pierre's missing result. What is this missing result? .................. [1] (ii) What effect does adding salt have on the boiling point of water? .................. [1] (c) Gabriella investigates change of state. She heats water gently and records the temperature every 2 minutes. The graph shows her results. (i) How many minutes does it take for the temperature of the water to reach 100 deg C? .................. minutes [1] (ii) Complete the sentences to explain what is happening to the water particles between 8 and 10 minutes. The water particles gain more .................. energy. The particles move .................. . This causes the particles to .................. out. [3] (iii) The diagram shows the particles in liquid water. Complete this diagram to show the particles in solid ice. [2]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) Tick: liquids have no fixed shape; particles in a liquid move around. (b)(i) 103°C. (b)(ii) Boiling point increases. (c)(i) read the time from the graph. (c)(ii) kinetic; faster; spread. (c)(iii) solid-ice particles touching / almost touching and arranged in regular rows.
(a) 勾选：液体没有固定形状；液体粒子会移动。(b)(i) 103°C。(b)(ii) 沸点升高。(c)(i) 按图读出到达 100°C 的时间。(c)(ii) kinetic、faster、spread。(c)(iii) 固态冰粒子互相接触或几乎接触，并排成规则行。
Accept · 可收：
  • liquids have no fixed shape
  • particles in a liquid move around
  • 103°C
  • 103 deg C
  • boiling point increases
  • boiling point goes up
  • correct graph reading for 100°C
  • kinetic
Q08State / Name3 marks
(b) Rajiv investigates the cooling curve of a substance. He measures the temperature of a hot liquid every minute. The graph shows his results. (i) What is the temperature of the hot liquid at the start? .................. deg C [1] (ii) Name the process that happens when a liquid changes into a solid. .................. [1] (iii) At what temperature does the liquid change into a solid? .................. deg C [1]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(b)(i) 81-82 deg C; (b)(ii) freezing; (b)(iii) 60 deg C.
(b)(i) 81-82 deg C; (b)(ii) freezing; (b)(iii) 60 deg C.
Accept · 可收：
  • (b)(i) 81-82 deg C
  • (b)(ii) freezing
  • (b)(iii) 60 deg C
  • (b)(i) 81-82 deg C; (b)(ii) freezing; (b)(iii) 60 deg C
  • (b)(i) 81-82 deg C.
  • (b)(ii) 凝固。
  • (b)(iii) 60 deg C.
Q09State / Identify2 marks
Lily investigates the boiling points of some liquids. (a) She uses a Bunsen burner to heat 20 cm3 of each liquid in a beaker. Which equipment should Lily use to measure the boiling point of the liquid? .................. [1] (b) Write down one safety precaution that Lily should take. .................. [1]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) thermometer; (b) one of: goggles / safety glasses; tie hair back; heatproof; gloves; apron / lab coat; stand up; use clamp / tongs.
(a) thermometer; (b) one of: goggles / safety glasses; tie hair back; heatproof; gloves; apron / lab coat; stand up; use clamp / tongs.
Accept · 可收：
  • (a) thermometer
  • (b) one of: goggles / safety glasses
  • tie hair back
  • heatproof
  • gloves
  • apron / lab coat
  • stand up
  • use clamp / tongs
Q10State4 marks
A heating curve for a pure substance has a flat section (a plateau) where the temperature stays the same for a while even though heating continues. (a) State what is happening to the substance during the flat section. .................. [1] (b) State what happens to the temperature during the flat section. .................. [1]
Expected answer · 标准答案
(a) A change of state is happening, such as melting or boiling. (b) The temperature stays constant / stays the same.
(a) 正在发生状态变化，例如融化或沸腾。(b) 温度保持不变。
Accept · 可收：
  • change of state is happening
  • melting is happening
  • boiling is happening
  • substance is changing state
  • temperature stays constant
  • temperature stays the same
  • temperature does not rise during the plateau
WRAP · Heating / Cooling Curves
加热 / 冷却曲线
WRAP
📝 SUMMARY · 总结
•
One idea ties this lesson together: a flat section (plateau) on a heating or cooling curve means a change of state is happening, and the temperature stays constant while it does.
一个想法把这一课串起来:加热或冷却曲线上的平台段表示正在发生状态变化,变化时温度保持不变。
•
This completes the whole unit's story: particles -> particle model -> atoms and elements -> periodic table -> elements / compounds / mixtures -> separating mixtures -> three states (arrangement, motion, attraction) -> changes of state -> heating and cooling curves.
这也把整个单元的故事讲完了:粒子 -> 粒子模型 -> 原子和元素 -> 周期表 -> 元素 / 化合物 /混合物 -> 分离混合物 -> 三态(排列、运动、吸引力)-> 状态变化
•
Next: what happens when there are no particles at all?
> 加热和冷却曲线。
•
That is a vacuum.
下一课:如果完全没有粒子会怎样?那就是真空。
🧠 MEMORY HOOK · 口诀金句
Flat line = state changing.
记忆法：Flat line = state changing。
🔁 COVER & RECALL · 盖住回想
A heating curve for a pure substance has a flat section (a plateau) where the temperature stays the same for a while even though heating continues. (a) State wh
(a) A change of state is happening, such as melting or boiling. (b) The temperature stays constant / stays the same.
(a) 正在发生状态变化，例如融化或沸腾。(b) 温度保持不变。
